title: Mismatched types
explanation: This error occurs when the compiler expected one type but found a different one. This is one of the most common Rust errors and can happen in many contexts: function return types, variable assignments, function arguments, and more.

fixes.description: Fix the return type to match the value
Convert the value to the expected type
Parse a string into the expected type

tip: Read the error message carefully — it tells you exactly which type was expected and which was found. The expected type comes from the context (annotation, return type, etc.).
